reasoning: Both models skipped the only critical file (PKGBUILD) by misclassifying it as binary, preventing any security analysis of build/install logic.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD skipped entirely despite being essential for understanding build/install behavior
Potential .install hooks or other scripts not evaluated due to cascading failure
false_negatives: All build/install risks missed: PKGBUILD could contain unsafe downloads, malicious commands, or unsafe permissions
Network/filesystem access patterns could not be assessed
learnings: Improve binary file detection: Never skip PKGBUILD without manual verification
Implement fallback: Treat PKGBUILD as text even with binary flags if extension matches
Prioritization failure: Critical files must override binary detection heuristics
Tool should fail explicitly when PKGBUILD is unreadable rather than skipping audit
